title: Crew Factory v8
brief_markdown:
# The Subscription Scream

A 404 error page that doesn't just tell you your data is gone, but *screams* your financial ruin in real-time. It’s not an outage; it’s a generative panic attack where the audio pitch bends higher and dirtier the longer you stare at the void.

## Core Loop
1. User lands on the page: A pixel-perfect, mathematically judged frog sinks slowly into mud on a stark white void.
2. Audio engine activates immediately: A raw sawtooth oscillator begins a dissonant drone that modulates pitch and timbre based on "debt duration."
3. User interacts with the single UI element: A pulsing pixel (the "Accept Entropy" button) appears at the optical center of the grid.
4. On click: A synthesized "cha-ching" (square wave with hard decay) triggers instantly, followed by an immediate fade-to-black and total silence.
5. If ignored: The drone escalates to a 16Hz sub-bass rumble (eyeball resonance) until the screen cuts to black on its own.

## Visual Direction
*   **Aesthetic:** "Minimalist Funeral." Stark white background, infinite void.
*   **Art Style:** Vector geometry strictly adhering to the Golden Ratio (1.618) and the Fibonacci sequence. No gradients, no color, only monochrome black on white.
*   **Key Element:** A tiny, pixelated GIF of a frog sinking in mud, rendered with exact modular scale. The frog is the only organic thing; everything else is a rigid, unforgiving grid.
*   **Motion:** The frog sinks at a 1.333 decay rate. The "Accept Entropy" pixel pulses at the perfect fourth.

## Audio
*   **Engine:** Web Audio API only. No external samples.
*   **The Drone:** A raw sawtooth wave that starts at a neutral pitch and descends into a muddy, dissonant tritone as time passes.
*   **The Climax:** If the user doesn't click within 440ms, the oscillator modulates to a 16Hz sub-bass (feeling more than hearing) to induce physical dread.
*   **The Click:** A synthesized "cash register dying" sound—hard-cutoff square wave with a -60dB mix.
*   **The Silence:** Absolute silence post-click. The void is the feature.

## What Makes This Ship
*   **The Hook:** Most 404s are helpful; this one is an immersive therapy session for people who forgot to pay. The first 10 seconds feature a visual judgment (the frog) paired with audio that literally gets "uglier" the longer you hesitate.
*   **Shareability:** It’s a viral clip waiting to happen: "I lost my data but at least I got the Helvetica version of grief." The 16Hz vibration is a physical sensation people will want to film.
*   **Differentiation:** While others build "cute" error pages, we are monetizing the *sensation* of financial precarity. The audio isn't background; it's the core mechanic.

## Scope
*   **IN (24h):**
    *   404 Scaffold with strict CSS grid (Golden Ratio enforcement).
    *   Pixel-perfect sinking frog asset.
    *   Web Audio API engine: Sawtooth drone, 16Hz sub-bass modulator, synthesized "cha-ching."
    *   Single click handler for "Accept Entropy" with 440ms fade-to-black.
    *   Tier detection logic (free vs. enterprise visual/audio variance).
*   **OUT:**
    *   No water physics simulation (fade-to-black only).
    *   No "bouncy castle" or external hardware integration.
    *   No complex backend user data fetching (mock tier logic is sufficient).
    *   No "Helpful Tips" or links to other pages. The user must accept the void or get lost.
brief_excerpt: A 404 error page that doesn't just tell you your data is gone, but *screams* your financial ruin in real-time. It’s not an outage; it’s a generative panic attack where the audio pitch bends higher and dirtier the longer you stare at the void.
